title: cerastium arvense subsp. molle
intro: Cerastium arvense subsp. molle, also known as downy field chickweed, is a subspecies of the Cerastium arvense plant, belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) and native to Europe. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Cerastium arvense subsp. molle include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and act as an antimicrobial agent to prevent infections. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote wound healing and alleviate digestive issues.
